behavior_summary: When the cobweb object is hit (U8P_COBWEB::gotHit event), the script records/marks a target with an internal info key and excludes it from further processing, then destroys the provided item/entity reference.
The primary visible gameplay effect is cleanup: removing the referenced item/entity from the world (via Item::destroy).
Internal 'set info' and 'process exclude' operations likely prevent further interactions or AI processing for the marked target after the hit.
notable_control_flow: Entry on Event 6 (U8P_COBWEB::gotHit) causes immediate state changes and a destroy call — no prolonged loops or waits are present in the excerpt.

The routine pushes a constant info id (observed 0x20C/0x210-style constant in related scripts) and the first parameter to perform the 'set info' / 'process exclude' operations before calling Item::destroy.
No global flags are read or written by this snippet; control flow is short and deterministic: mark/exclude -> destroy -> return/suspend.
open_questions: Is the destroyed item (BP+06h) the cobweb itself or the entity that struck the cobweb? The disassembly shows the first parameter is destroyed but calling convention context is not present in the excerpt.
What exact semantic meaning corresponds to the info id constant (0x20C / 0x210 inferred from related scripts)?
Does 'process exclude' prevent only AI processing or also player interactions and collisions for the marked object?
Is the second parameter (BP+08h / param_1) ever used in other variants of this handler (it is unused in the provided excerpt)?

main_function.name: U8P_COBWEB::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Handle the cobweb being struck: set an internal info/state on a target and exclude that target from further processing, then destroy the passed-in item/entity.
entry_conditions: Event 6 (U8P_COBWEB::gotHit) — invoked when this cobweb process/object is hit by something

function_map.name: U8P_COBWEB::gotHit
function_map.role: Event handler for the cobweb 'U8P_COBWEB::gotHit' event; updates target info/exclusion state and destroys the referenced item/entity

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Engine/item API call to remove/destroy an item or object instance from the game world

internal_calls.purpose: Attach/record an info/state value (observed constant pushed) on a target object so the engine or scripts can treat it differently
Exclude the marked object from normal processing (likely prevents further AI/interaction); paired with the set_info call

external_calls.purpose: Remove/destroy the item/entity whose reference was passed in (parameter at BP+06h). This is the primary observable effect of the handler.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the cobweb object is hit (U8P_COBWEB::gotHit event), the script records/marks a target with an internal info key and excludes it from further processing, then destroys the provided item/entity reference.
